sections.paragraph:
δεσπότις, ιδος, ἡ (Soph., Eur., Pla. et al.; SEG VIII, 548, 25 [Isisaretal. I a.d.] term used by a slave in address to an owner POxy 48, 7; Sb 5616, 6) mistress [ἡ τ]ο̣ῦ̣ χρυσοῦ πολλοῦ δ. mistress of much gold AcPl Ha 2, 19.—DELG s.v. δεσπότης.

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SEG SEG = Supplementum Epigraphicum Graecum—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 220
